source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Ansistrano Deployments: Deploying the web server and the software

questions.q: Install the geerlingguy.apache role with ansible-galaxy.
questions.why: geerlingguy.apache installs and configures Apache for you.

questions.q: Why install the ansible.posix collection in this lab?
options: For its firewalld module, to open the firewall for http
questions.why: ansible.posix provides the firewalld module used to permit the http service.

questions.q: Install the ansistrano.deploy role with ansible-galaxy.
questions.why: ansistrano.deploy is the role that performs the deployment.

questions.q: Which variable tells Ansistrano the download method (e.g. git)?
Which variable sets the git repository URL to deploy?
questions.why: ansistrano_git_repo is the source repository URL.

questions.q: Which variable sets the destination path to deploy into?
questions.why: ansistrano_deploy_to (here "{{ dest }}") is the deployment target directory.

questions.q: After configuring Apache but before deploying code, curl returns HTTP 404. Why is that expected?
options: No code is deployed yet, but httpd runs and the firewall is open
questions.why: The 404 confirms the service works and the port is open; there is simply no content yet.
